questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A 100 cm long cylindrical flask with inner and outer radius $r_1=2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $r_2=4 \mathrm{~cm}$ respectively, is completely filled with ice at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ as shown in the figure. The constant temperature outside (surrounding) the flask is $40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Assume heat exchange occurs only through the curved surface of the flask. (Thermal conductivity of the flask is $0.693 \mathrm{~W} . \mathrm{m}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{L}_{\text {ice }}=80 \mathrm{cal} / \mathrm{gm}$ and $\left.1 \mathrm{cal}=4200 \mathrm{~J}\right)$.
options: Rate of heat flow from surrounding to the flask is $80 \pi \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{s}$.
The rate at which ice melts is $\frac{\pi}{4200} \mathrm{Kg} / \mathrm{s}$.
The rate at which ice melts is $100 \pi \mathrm{Kg} / \mathrm{s}$.
Rate of heat flow from surrounding to the flask is $40 \pi \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{s}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}=\frac{K A d \theta}{d r}=\mathrm{K}(2 \pi \mathrm{rL}) \frac{d \theta}{d r} \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{H} \int_{r_1}^{r_2} \frac{d r}{r}=2 \pi K L\left(\theta_2-\theta_1\right)=80 \pi \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{d m}{d t} L=80 \pi \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{d m}{d t}=\frac{8 \pi}{L}=\frac{80 \pi}{80 \times 4200}=\frac{\pi}{4200} \mathrm{Kg} / \text { second. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two cylindrical vessels, each having length 10 m and area of cross-sectional area of $1 \mathrm{~m}^2$, are filled with water and rotated with angular speed $\omega=20 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$. Then, which of the following statement is correct?
options: If both vessels are stopped suddenly, water in vessel A will be at higher temperature.
If both vessels are stopped suddenly, water in vessel B will be at higher temperature
Change in temperature of water in vessel A is $1.58^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
Change in temperature of water in vessel A is $1.3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
questions.solution:
A has higher kinetic energy than $B$.
$\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~mL}^2 \omega^2=\mathrm{mS} \Delta \mathrm{~T}$
$\Delta \mathrm{T}=\frac{\mathrm{L}^2 \omega^2}{3 \mathrm{~s}}=\frac{10^2 \times 20^2}{3 \times 4.2 \times 10^3}=\frac{20}{3 \times 4.2}=1.58^{\circ}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Four rods $A, B, C, D$ of same length and material but of different radii $r, r \sqrt{2}, r \sqrt{3}$ and $2 r$ respectively are held between two rigid walls. The temperature of all rods is increased by same amount. If the rods do not bend, then
options: the stress in the rods are in the ratio $1: 2: 3: 4$.
the force on the rods exerted by the wall are in the ratio $1: 2: 3: 4$.
the energy stored in the rods due to elasticity are in the ratio $1: 2: 3: 4$.
the strains produced in the rods are in the ratio $1: 2: 3: 4$.
questions.solution:
Strain will remain same.
Stress $=Y \alpha \Delta \theta$ will remain same.
Force $=\mathrm{YA} \alpha \Delta \theta$
Energy $=\frac{1}{2}($ stress $)($ strain $)($ volume $)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
